titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 591

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=820 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.761233+00 | translation moa=821 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 14:52:15.749652+00

div.p:
W Rex Scott’ . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . et Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue  salutem . preceipio fir
miter . ut ubique seruiens Monachorum de Goldingham inuenire poterit fugitiuos . et natiuos eorum . de
Goldinghamshire . siue aliarum terrarum suarum  eos iuste habeat cum omnibus rebus . et catallis eorum . et prohibeo . ne quis illos iniuste detineat  super meam forisfacturam T’ Nicholao Canecellario . Matheo
archidiacono . apud Forfare

p.cl: William, king of the Scots, to justices, sheriffs, and all worthy men of the whole of his land, greeting.

I resolutely instruct that wherever a tenant of the monks of Coldingham may discover fugitives and neyfs of Coldinghamshire or of their other lands, he is legitimately to have them, with all things, and their chattels, and I forbid that anyone unlawfully detain them, on pain of my forefeiture.
As witnesses: Nicholas the Chancellor, Matthew the Archdeacon.
